tests.name: rule engine invariants (property-based) never throws and always emits well-formed diagnostics
rule engine invariants (property-based) is deterministic: same design, same diagnostics
rule engine invariants (property-based) 'off' config removes exactly that rule's diagnostics and nothing else
rule engine invariants (property-based) every emitted code round-trips through the numeric mapping
metamorphic laws adding a GND wire never INTRODUCES a missing-ground-return error
metamorphic laws fully specifying every wire eliminates all MFG missing-field diagnostics
metamorphic laws parseAwg inverts rendering for every integer gauge 1..40
parseAwg accepts the common spellings
parseAwg rejects non-gauges
parseAwg rejects zero (and therefore aught sizes — 4/0AWG is out of scope)
parseAwg documents the decimal quirk: '20.5AWG' parses as 5AWG
ampacity table is monotonic: thicker wire always carries more
ampacity table requiredAwgForCurrent picks the thinnest sufficient gauge
signal classifiers power: rails and voltage names
signal classifiers ground is token-aware: prefixed and suffixed grounds classify
signal classifiers shield: unanchored, so prefixed drains match
differentialPartner CAN with and without underscore, with bus prefixes
differentialPartner RS-485, generic _P/_N, USB
differentialPartner non-pairs return undefined
HK-DOC-001 missingRevision fires on whitespace-only revision
HK-DOC-001 missingRevision passes with a real revision
HK-DOC-002 branchMissingLabel fires for an unlabeled branch with the branch target
HK-DOC-002 branchMissingLabel passes when a label attaches to the branch
HK-DOC-003 spliceMissingNotes fires when type, part, and notes are all absent
HK-DOC-003 spliceMissingNotes passes with any one of type / notes
HK-MFG-001/002/003 missing length/color/gauge each fires independently
HK-MFG-001/002/003 missing length/color/gauge a fully specified wire passes all three
HK-MFG-001/002/003 missing length/color/gauge length is a warning; color and gauge are errors
HK-MFG-004 gaugeOutsideConnectorRange (range 30AWG..18AWG) boundaries are inclusive: exactly min and exactly max pass
HK-MFG-004 gaugeOutsideConnectorRange (range 30AWG..18AWG) one step outside fires at both endpoints of the wire
HK-MFG-004 gaugeOutsideConnectorRange (range 30AWG..18AWG) skips wires without a parseable gauge and connectors without a range
HK-WIRE-004 gaugeCurrentMismatch exactly at ampacity passes; just over fires
HK-WIRE-004 gaugeCurrentMismatch current beyond the whole table says so
HK-WIRE-004 gaugeCurrentMismatch skips wires without an estimate or with an off-table gauge
HK-ELEC-001 differentialPairNotTwisted fires when neither wire is twisted, and when groups differ
HK-ELEC-001 differentialPairNotTwisted passes when the pair shares a twist group
HK-ELEC-001 differentialPairNotTwisted a lone CAN_H with no CAN_L present is not a pair violation
HK-ELEC-002 twistGroupTooSmall a singleton group fires; a pair passes; two singletons sort deterministically
HK-ELEC-003 missingGroundReturn power without ground fires, listing unique sorted rails
HK-ELEC-003 missingGroundReturn passes with a ground wire, and with no power at all
HK-ELEC-004 shieldDrainUnconnected (+ HK-CONN-010 exclusion) fires as a warning for an unwired shield pin; CONN-010 stays silent for it
HK-ELEC-004 shieldDrainUnconnected (+ HK-CONN-010 exclusion) passes once the drain lands on a wire
HK-CONN-010 unconnectedAssignedPin fires for an assigned, unwired pin; unassigned pins are fine
HK-CONN-010 unconnectedAssignedPin a wire to a splice counts as wiring the pin
HK-CONN-011 wireSignalMismatch fires once per mismatched endpoint
HK-CONN-011 wireSignalMismatch passes on matching signals; skips unassigned pins and signalless wires
rule engine severity can be overridden per rule without changing the code
rule engine output is deterministic and canonically ordered by (target, code, message)
rule engine a report-level code overrides the rule's code
per-pin terminals and seals (PRD §30) carries terminal/seal assignments into HIR pins and the BOM
per-pin terminals and seals (PRD §30) HK-CONN-012: terminal not in the part's compatible list
per-pin terminals and seals (PRD §30) HK-CONN-013: sealed connector with unsealed populated pin
per-pin terminals and seals (PRD §30) HK-CONN-014: seal not in the part's compatible list
per-pin terminals and seals (PRD §30) a fully sealed, compatible assignment passes all built-ins
approvals and provenance (PRD §30 acceptance) requireApprovedParts flags BOM items off the org list without mutating library data
approvals and provenance (PRD §30 acceptance) library parts carry provenance into HIR
rule code numbers round-trips every built-in rule code
rule code numbers built-in numbers are unique and sort by category band
rule code numbers custom and malformed codes return undefined (strings stay the contract)
rule code numbers category math works from either representation
rule code numbers codesToNumbers dedupes, sorts, and drops custom codes
differentialPartner anchors (regex mutants) does not pair signals with trailing garbage (kills $-drop mutants)
differentialPartner anchors (regex mutants) pairs the no-underscore spellings (kills _?-tightening mutants)
differentialPartner anchors (regex mutants) bus index must be digits (kills \d->\D mutant)
signal classification edges decimal voltage rails are power (kills \d+->\d in POWER_SIGNAL)
parseAwg edges rejects zero and keeps whitespace tolerance
rule-code numeric boundaries suffix 999 stays in band; 1000 is impossible by grammar
